questions.question: How many charts can a user with a Free Plan create?

options.explanation: Unlimited charts are available only on a Paid plan. Free Plan members are limited to one chart.
The Free Plan limit is one chart, not five.
Correct. Free Plan members can create one chart for free.
The Free Plan limit is one chart, not ten.

questions.question: Which slash command is used to create a new chart view in Notion?

options.explanation: /database creates a new database page, not a chart view.
Correct. The slash command '/chart' opens the menu to select your chart type.
/view is a general command that opens the view menu, but /chart is specific to creating charts.
/table creates a table view, which is a different database view type.

questions.question: Which of the following actions is NOT available when viewing a chart drilldown?

options.text: Copy the link to the chart
options.explanation: You can copy the link to the chart even within a drilldown view.

options.text: Select multiple rows and take bulk actions
options.explanation: Correct. Drilldown views currently do not support selecting multiple rows for bulk actions.

options.text: View data in a table view
options.explanation: Drilldowns currently appear as table views, so this is available.

options.text: Save the drilldown as a view
options.explanation: You can save a drilldown as a view by selecting 'Save as view in'.

questions.question: Which data type is NOT supported on the X or Y axis of a bar or line chart?

options.explanation: Rollups are generally supported for the Y axis to show aggregated data.
Count is a valid option for the Y axis.
Correct. Buttons, unique IDs, files, and media are not supported on axes.
Properties are the standard data type used for axes.

questions.question: If your database’s sub-items are not reflecting in your chart, what setting must you adjust in the database settings?

options.explanation: Sorting order does not affect whether sub-items are displayed in the chart.
Grouping determines how data is organized, not how sub-items are flattened.
Correct. You must change the 'Show as' setting to 'Flattened list' in More settings to display all items.
While the setting is found under Sub-items or Sub-tasks, the specific toggle to fix the display is 'Show as'.

questions.question: What is the purpose of the 'Omit zero values' toggle on the X or Y axis?

options.text: To hide groups that have no data
options.explanation: Correct. If you toggle this on, groups with zero associated items will not appear in the chart.

options.text: To prevent the chart from crashing if data is missing
options.explanation: This toggle controls visibility, not stability or crash prevention.

options.text: To sort the data so that zero values appear first
options.explanation: This toggle controls visibility, not sorting order.

options.text: To remove the axis title if the value is zero
options.explanation: The axis title is controlled by the 'Axis name' setting, not 'Omit zero values'.

questions.question: What condition must be met for the 'Cumulative' toggle to appear on the Y axis?

options.text: The chart type is a Donut chart
options.explanation: The Cumulative toggle is specific to bar and line charts, not Donut charts.

options.text: The Y axis is showing a formula
options.explanation: The Cumulative toggle is available when Y axis is showing Count or Sum.

options.text: The X axis property is sorted in ascending order
options.explanation: Correct. The toggle appears only if you are showing Count or Sum and the X axis is sorted ascending.

options.text: The chart is showing data labels
options.explanation: Data labels are a separate style setting and do not enable the Cumulative toggle.

questions.question: Which style option makes the line in a line chart curved instead of angular?

options.explanation: Correct. Toggling 'Smooth line' turns the line into a curve.
Gradient area fills the space under the line with a gradient, it does not smooth the line itself.
Grid lines add reference lines to the background, they don't affect line curvature.
Color by value changes the color intensity based on value, not the shape of the line.

questions.question: Can you edit database entries directly from a chart view?

options.text: Yes, you can edit them like in a table view
options.explanation: Currently, you cannot edit database entries from the chart view itself.

options.text: No, you must switch to another view to edit data
options.explanation: Correct. Chart view is for visualization only; you must use another view to edit data.
This limitation applies to all platforms.

options.text: Only if you are an admin
options.explanation: Editing permissions are not the issue; chart view simply does not support input.

questions.question: Which download options are currently unavailable on the Android app?

options.explanation: Copy as PNG is available on Android.
Download PNG is available on Android.
Correct. Downloading as SVG is not currently available on the Android app.
Save chart as... is available on Android.

questions.question: How can you exclude specific groups (e.g., completed tasks) from a chart visualization?

options.text: By setting 'Show as' to Hidden list
options.explanation: There is no 'Hidden list' option in the database settings.

options.text: By clicking the eye icon next to the option in Visible groups
options.explanation: Correct. Selecting the eye icon hides the group and moves it to 'Hidden groups'.
Omit zero values hides groups with zero data, not specific groups you choose to exclude.

options.text: By changing the chart type to Donut
options.explanation: Changing the chart type changes the visualization, it doesn't filter specific groups.

questions.question: Which feature allows you to remove the 'Made with Notion' watermark when saving a chart?

options.explanation: Changing the background color does not remove the watermark.
This setting controls color intensity, not watermarks.

options.text: Choosing not to include a background on a Paid plan
options.explanation: Correct. On a Paid plan, you can choose to have no background, which removes the watermark.

options.text: Selecting 'No watermark' in the style menu
options.explanation: There is no specific 'No watermark' toggle; it is a side effect of removing the background on a Paid plan.

questions.question: Where can you add a Chart view to an existing database?

options.explanation: Chart settings are only available after you have added the view.

options.text: By clicking the + next to the database name
options.explanation: Correct. Click the + and select Chart from the dropdown to add the view.
Adding views is done at the database level, not the property level.

options.text: Only by using the slash command
options.explanation: You can also add a chart view via the dropdown menu next to the database name.

questions.question: What is the maximum number of groups a chart can display at one time?

options.explanation: 50 is the limit for subgroups, not groups.
The limit is higher than 100.
Correct. Charts can display up to 200 groups at a time.
The limit is 200 groups and 50 subgroups.

questions.question: What is the main difference in configuration options between a Donut chart and a Bar/Line chart?

options.text: Donut charts cannot use the X and Y axis configuration
options.explanation: Both chart types use axis configuration; Donut charts use a 'Data' section instead.

options.text: Bar and Line charts cannot be customized with style options like Smooth line
options.explanation: Bar and Line charts support many style options that Donut charts do not, such as Smooth line and Gradient area.

options.text: Donut charts use a 'Data' section to configure what to show and how slices are organized
options.explanation: Correct. While Bar/Line charts use X/Y axes, Donut charts use a 'Data' section to define properties.

options.text: Bar and Line charts do not support the 'Omit zero values' toggle
options.explanation: Both Bar/Line and Donut charts support the 'Omit zero values' toggle.
